data.narrative:
Fortastra, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company founded by Mike Smayda, has raised $8M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of five private-market peers in identical sector and stage (Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace, and Loft Orbital) and five public-market anchors (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. Stage-progression methodology, using $95M as a total-raised proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yields $162M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $1.1B, with observed valuations ranging from $1.0B to $8.0B. Public-market anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median: 42.9x across six tickers) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures. The triangulated analysis across both applicable methods supports a valuation range of $137M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $656M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-specific valuation uplift and meaningful heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the $8.0B outlier (Sierra Space) relative to the $1.0B–$1.5B clustering. Revenue scale and differentiation within the target's specific subsector remain key value drivers.
